evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoint wss://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id}/stream-input and xi-api-key header are internally consistent. However, websockets.connect uses extra_headers= which is deprecated/removed in newer websockets versions (now additional_headers). No version pinning. generation_config/output_format placement within init message is plausible but not verified. Long-tail: internally consistent, partial version claims — score 3.
evidence.executability: Multiple fixes needed: elevenlabs_client.py reads env vars at import time but main.py loads dotenv after importing — actually elevenlabs_client is imported later so may work, but env vars in elevenlabs_client.py module level still execute on import. No requirements.txt or pip install bash block. No .env template fenced block. Frontend has no package.json/install commands. WAV-per-chunk strategy is mentioned as potentially problematic in the prose itself. extra_headers kwarg may break on newer websockets lib.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition described in prose, not crisp; (b) NO ```bash install block for pip or npm — major gap; (c) env var names listed in a table but no .env fenced template; (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test code present but no runnable bash test command. Missing install commands and .env template are critical extractor gaps.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare bracket numbers like [10][27] with no URLs. No canonical elevenlabs.io/docs URLs anywhere. No hyperlinks. Extractor cannot resolve references. Only inline numbers, no bibliography.

rubric_notes: Very long, prose-heavy. No bash install fences, no .env fenced template, no resolvable URLs. Code blocks are present and substantial but extractor would miss installs and env scaffold.
